author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Research-backed design:
- Scan repository root, not .agents/pm, for AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Treat missing files as eligible targets only when the user explicitly asks to add guidance or approves creation.
- Positive detection should require either a stable pm marker or a minimum set of command/workflow tokens: PM_AUTHOR, pm context, pm search, pm create, pm claim, pm files, pm docs, pm test, pm test --run, pm close, pm release.
- Writer should append a concise marked section, for example <!-- pm-cli-guidance:start v1 --> ... <!-- pm-cli-guidance:end -->, and update only the marked block on future template-version changes.
- Guidance content should be short enough for startup context: bootstrap, orient/search before create, claim/update, link files/docs/tests, evidence comments, linked test run, close/release, TOON/JSON guidance, and sandbox-safe tests.
- Preserve existing line endings where practical and use atomic writes plus extension onWrite hooks if available in the chosen implementation path.
Build idempotent AGENTS/CLAUDE pm guidance detector and writer

Create the reusable logic that inspects root AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md, decides whether core pm guidance is present, and appends or creates a stable marked guidance block without overwriting user content.

Detector reports present/missing/outdated per AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md; writer is idempotent; existing user content is preserved; missing files are created only on explicit approval/add mode; marked block can be updated by template version; unit tests cover present, missing, outdated, absent-file, and both-file scenarios.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Child task created from pm-7t04 detailed research. This slice isolates filesystem/template behavior before init prompt wiring.

message: Create child task for guidance detector and writer
